title: Code Points, Grapheme Clusters, Character Categories, and Labels · Behavior Case: Grapheme Transfer
summary: A report labels len(text) as characters, counts newline and combining marks as letters, merges case categories without policy, and presents incomparable metrics under one total. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the grapheme transfer increment in honestly labeled text-metrics panel. Learners progress through p…
objectives: Name the standard-library limitation for full grapheme segmentation and require a pinned evaluated implementation in native transfer.
State whether character frequencies operate on original or normalized text, preserve case, and include or exclude marks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-character-grapheme-metrics-5
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-character-grapheme-metrics-5.
# Case 9036: a digital-humanities corpus revision.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases.
def worked_bookbot_character_grapheme_metrics_5(code_points: int = 14, graphemes: int = 11, letters: int = 9, labels_explicit: bool = True, segmentation_transfer_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "corpusworked5",
        "policy5dekrow",
        "revision9036",
        "commandworked59036",
        "report5dekrow9036",
        "test9036worked5",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if min(code_points, graphemes, letters) < 0:
        return False, code_points, "metric-count"
    if graphemes > code_points or letters > code_points:
        return False, graphemes, "unit-reconciliation"
    if not labels_explicit:
        return False, letters, "ambiguous-unit"
    if not segmentation_transfer_named:
        return False, graphemes, "grapheme-overclaim"
    assert letters <= code_points
    return True, graphemes, "honest-text-metrics"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-grapheme-transfer-1yhk07
# Competency: bookbot-grapheme-transfer.
# Case 3483: a clean-environment package release.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects.
# Changed case: replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts.
def evidence_bookbot_grapheme_transfer_1yhk07(code_points: int = 14, graphemes: int = 11, letters: int = 9, labels_explicit: bool = True, segmentation_transfer_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1yhk07 = "1yhk07-70khy1-3483"
    assert evidence_variant_1yhk07
    evidence_axes_1yhk07 = (
        "corpus1yhk07",
        "policy70khy1",
        "revision3483",
        "command1yhk073483",
        "report70khy13483",
        "test34831yhk07",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1yhk07)) == 6
    if min(code_points, graphemes, letters) < 0:
        return False, code_points, "metric-count"
    if graphemes > code_points or letters > code_points:
        return False, graphemes, "unit-reconciliation"
    if not labels_explicit:
        return False, letters, "ambiguous-unit"
    if not segmentation_transfer_named:
        return False, graphemes, "grapheme-overclaim"
    assert letters <= code_points
    return True, graphemes, "honest-text-metrics"

steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Bookbot and Python Defend the Grapheme Transfer model · Theory Grapheme Transfer, keep Grapheme Transfer connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Name the standard-library limitation for full grapheme segmentation and require a pinned evaluated implementation in native transfer.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Grapheme Transfer model · Theory Grapheme Transfer, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Grapheme Transfer precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Grapheme Transfer model · Theory Grapheme Transfer, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 8499 during a clean-environment package release. Build deterministic Python evidence to label and reconcile code-point, grapheme, and letter metrics without overstating browser segmentation; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then change LF to CRLF, remove the final newline, and preserve the declared word and line policy. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Name the standard-library limitation for full grapheme segmentation and require a pinned evaluated implementation in native transfer.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Grapheme Transfer model · Theory Grapheme Transfer, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Python slicing cannot divide a user-perceived character.
